fact: 🌎 Gray wolves are the largest wild members of the dog family, Canidae.
🌎 Gray wolves once had one of the broadest natural ranges of any land mammal in the Northern Hemisphere.
🌎 Arctic wolves are a gray wolf subspecies adapted to the cold High Arctic.
🌎 Arctic wolves often have pale coats that help them blend into snowy landscapes.
🌎 Arctic wolf prey can include muskoxen, Arctic hares, and caribou depending on location.
🌎 The Mexican wolf is the rarest subspecies of gray wolf in North America.
🌎 Mexican wolves are also known by the Spanish name lobos.
🌎 Mexican wolves were reintroduced to parts of Arizona and New Mexico beginning in 1998.
🌎 Mexican wolves were listed as endangered in the United States in 1976.
🌎 Red wolves are native to the southeastern United States.
🌎 Red wolves are smaller and slimmer than many gray wolves but larger than coyotes.
🌎 Red wolves are one of the most endangered canids in the world.
🌎 Wild red wolves currently survive only in a small recovery area in eastern North Carolina.
🌎 Ethiopian wolves live in the highlands of Ethiopia and are Africa's most endangered carnivore.
🌎 Ethiopian wolves specialize in hunting rodents in Afroalpine grasslands.
🌎 Ethiopian wolves are more closely related to gray wolves and coyotes than to jackals, despite their foxlike build.
🌎 Eurasian wolves are gray wolves found across parts of Europe and Asia.
🌎 Italian wolves are a gray wolf population associated with the Apennine Mountains and wider recolonization of Europe.
🌎 Iberian wolves are found mainly in Spain and Portugal.
🌎 Indian wolves are adapted to warmer, drier landscapes than many northern wolf populations.
🌎 Himalayan wolves are adapted to high-altitude environments in parts of Central and South Asia.
🌎 Tundra wolves are large northern gray wolves associated with Arctic and subarctic tundra regions.
🌎 Northwestern wolves are among the larger gray wolf forms in North America.
🌎 Mackenzie Valley wolves are famous for their large size and northern range.
🌎 Alexander Archipelago wolves live in coastal rainforest regions of southeast Alaska.
🌎 Alexander Archipelago wolves are closely tied to deer and coastal island ecosystems.
🌎 The Honshu wolf of Japan is extinct and was one of the smallest recognized gray wolf forms.
🌎 The Hokkaido wolf, also called the Ezo wolf, is extinct and once lived in northern Japan.
🌎 Dire wolves were real Ice Age canids, but they were not simply oversized gray wolves.
🌎 Dire wolves went extinct thousands of years ago and are known from fossils across the Americas.
🌎 Coyotes are close relatives of wolves but are a separate species.
🌎 The maned wolf of South America is not a true wolf despite its name.
🌎 The aardwolf is not a wolf at all; it is a hyena relative that eats termites.
